title: DoorDash pops 12% on strong earnings, upbeat order growth guidance
text: DoorDash reported Q1 2026 earnings with an EPS of $0.42, surpassing the forecast of $0.37, marking a 13.51% positive surprise. Total orders hit a record 933 million, up 27% year over year, with GOV up 37% to $31.6 billion. The company projected Q2 2026 Marketplace Gross Order Value of $32.4 billion to $33.4 billion, with a midpoint of roughly $32.9 billion, well above the $28.0 billion analysts had been modelling. Full-year EPS guidance of $5.62 reassured investors who had worried that integration costs from Deliveroo would compress profits. DoorDash maintains a positive EBITDA outlook for 2026, expecting margins to improve compared to 2025, and anticipates higher profitability in the second half of the year. The strong guidance significantly exceeded analyst expectations and contributed to a substantial rally in DoorDash stock following the earnings announcement.
